reviewTrigger: control finding, false positive, fixture drift, owner change, command change, or accepted-outcome regression

controls.failureClass: Effect service, error, environment, and diagnostic contract drift
controls.invariant: Effect code remains typed and diagnostics remain enabled at authoring and verification time.
controls.admissionEvidence: The language-service gate and boundary fixtures prevent recurring typed-Effect regressions before package tests.
controls.falsePositiveCost: A false positive blocks local verification and requires a narrow compiler/diagnostic reproduction.
controls.repairCost: Usually one owning Schema, service, or diagnostic correction; never a blanket suppression.
controls.carryingCost: Maintain the pinned compiler integration and requalify after TypeScript or Effect upgrades.
controls.reviewTrigger: TypeScript, Effect, language-service, service, error, or diagnostic change.
controls.retirement: Retire only when an earlier compiler-owned control proves the same failures with equal fixtures.
controls.rollback: Restore the last passing TypeScript/Effect setup and retain the failed upgrade evidence.

metricPlan.acceptedOutcome: Count accepted fixes attributable to the diagnostic.
metricPlan.falsePositive: Record disputed diagnostics per changed source set.
metricPlan.synchronousHumanAttention: Record review minutes needed to classify disputed output.
metricPlan.workerDuration: Record focused correction duration, not command volume.

controls.nonClaim: A patched compiler does not prove runtime, provider, or Production behavior.

controls.failureClass: unchecked inbound/outbound values, raw semantic primitives, unsafe codecs, assertions, or framework adapter leakage
controls.invariant: Encoding and decoding occur once at named boundaries and exceptions match exact file, symbol, rule, and occurrence.
controls.admissionEvidence: Positive and negative fixtures cover exact admission, occurrence isolation, duplicates, stale entries, codecs, Config, JSON, HTTP, persistence, and assertions.
controls.falsePositiveCost: An exact diagnostic blocks one occurrence; resolution requires an owner-named contract or a justified exact exception.
controls.repairCost: Usually local to the adapter; repeated exceptions are retained until a proven equivalent representation exists.
controls.carryingCost: Maintain compiler-API matching and 18 exact occurrence records.
controls.reviewTrigger: Boundary syntax, TypeScript AST, exception, provider SDK, or source-condition change.
controls.retirement: Replace only with equal exact-match, duplicate, occurrence-isolation, and stale-detection evidence.
controls.rollback: Restore the last exact registry and audit together; never widen to file- or symbol-only admission.

metricPlan.acceptedOutcome: Count escaped boundary failures prevented or corrected.
metricPlan.falsePositive: Record exact diagnostics rejected after owner/call-graph review.
metricPlan.feedbackLatency: Measure source change to bounded diagnostic.
metricPlan.workerDuration: Record correction duration per diagnostic class.

controls.nonClaim: Registry line-count reduction is not effectiveness or equivalence.

controls.failureClass: static correctness, consistency, tagged-error identity, and format drift
controls.invariant: Type-aware Ultracite, repository lint rules, and formatting reject their named failure classes.
controls.admissionEvidence: The repository lint fixture proves exact tagged-error-name failures and the type-aware command covers current source.
controls.falsePositiveCost: A false positive blocks the changed file and requires a minimal lint fixture before policy changes.
controls.repairCost: Local source or rule-fixture correction; no broad disable without a SPEC-owned replacement.
controls.carryingCost: Maintain configuration and focused rule fixtures across tool upgrades.
controls.reviewTrigger: Lint, formatting, generated source, TypeScript, or tagged-error change.
controls.retirement: Retire a rule when it no longer prevents its named failure or a stronger earlier owner replaces it.
controls.rollback: Restore the prior rule/config and retain the rejected fixture.

metricPlan.falsePositive: Track suppressions proposed and rejected/accepted with fixtures.
metricPlan.workerDuration: Record fix duration for each rule class.

controls.nonClaim: A clean lint result does not prove business behavior or external state.

controls.failureClass: stale current owners, broken lifecycle routes, duplicated truth, unsafe freshness publication, and provider-actuality prose
controls.invariant: Documentation updates the earliest durable owner; background freshness remains isolated report-only input until distinct review/publication/readback.
controls.admissionEvidence: Semantic owner fixtures plus typed freshness candidates reject self-source, self-review, self-publication, incomplete publication, and candidate-root ownership.
controls.falsePositiveCost: A false positive requires an exact owner/claim segment or candidate fixture; counts alone cannot waive it.
controls.repairCost: Update the canonical owner and necessary pointers, or quarantine the candidate with recovery data.
controls.carryingCost: Maintain lifecycle metadata, semantic claim fixtures, and the freshness contract.
controls.reviewTrigger: Current owner, claim, route, lifecycle, background scan, publication, or recovery change.
controls.retirement: Retire only when a smaller generated owner provides the same semantic, lifecycle, and publication safety evidence.
controls.rollback: Restore the last-known-good owner and quarantine the candidate or policy revision.

metricPlan.acceptedOutcome: Count candidates accepted into the correct owner, rejected, or quarantined.
metricPlan.falsePositive: Record reviewer-rejected candidate findings by class.
metricPlan.feedbackLatency: Measure source observation to reviewer decision.
metricPlan.workerDuration: Record scan and correction duration separately.

controls.nonClaim: A candidate, documentation check, or publisher receipt does not prove provider actuality.

controls.failureClass: missing, broken, contradictory, non-portable, or stale repository skill guidance
controls.invariant: Required skills and mirrors remain complete, portable, metadata-valid, and semantically aligned with executable owners.
controls.admissionEvidence: Skill-policy fixtures reject missing routes, invalid metadata, contradictory examples, fixed rituals, broken mirrors, and non-portable paths.
controls.falsePositiveCost: A false positive blocks a skill surface and requires a semantic-term or mirror fixture.
controls.repairCost: Update the skill owner, UI metadata, mirror, and exact policy fixture atomically.
controls.carryingCost: Maintain six required mirrors and policy fragments when owners move.
controls.reviewTrigger: Skill, mirror, agent instruction, owner path, executable example, or metadata change.
controls.retirement: Retire a reminder after its stronger schema/lint/test owner and pointer are accepted.
controls.rollback: Restore the last complete mirror set and retained source revision.

metricPlan.acceptedOutcome: Track skill defects corrected before implementation.
metricPlan.falsePositive: Record policy matches rejected after semantic review.
metricPlan.feedbackLatency: Measure skill edit to bounded finding.
metricPlan.workerDuration: Record correction duration per skill surface.

controls.nonClaim: Valid skill text does not prove a worker followed it or a provider behaved accordingly.

controls.failureClass: unbounded workflow identity, permissions, triggers, convergence, actions, or tool-derived authority
controls.invariant: Workflow desired source and interaction/provider envelopes are exact, least-privilege, bounded, convergent, and non-authorizing without readback.
controls.admissionEvidence: Static negative fixtures cover inventory, permissions, target, timeout, convergence, pins, readback, containment, and retired review.
controls.falsePositiveCost: A false positive blocks desired workflow source and requires an exact YAML/register fixture.
controls.repairCost: Correct source/register/lock together; external application remains separately approved.
controls.carryingCost: Maintain three workflow contracts, 15 authority rows, and five action pins.
controls.reviewTrigger: Workflow, action, permission, OIDC, token, target, automation, or external-setting change.
controls.retirement: Retire a capability when admission or accepted-outcome evidence is absent; restore only through a new accepted SPEC.
controls.rollback: Restore the prior source/register/lock and separately reverse external state if authorized.

metricPlan.acceptedOutcome: Track accepted workflow outcomes, not run count.
metricPlan.falsePositive: Track rejected checks/comments or duplicate actions.
metricPlan.workerDuration: Record run duration separately from human latency.

controls.nonClaim: Desired source and local fixtures do not prove GitHub settings, runs, credentials, or consequences.

controls.failureClass: false success, ambiguous artifact, missing authority/readback, evidence tamper, or unbounded output
controls.invariant: Proof identifies candidate, journey, environment, authority, consequence, evidence, limitations, recovery, and immutable lifecycle.
controls.admissionEvidence: Strict schemas and semantic fixtures reject false success, tamper, missing detail, unsafe rerun, unbounded output, and unavailable-as-proved readback.
controls.falsePositiveCost: A false positive blocks one packet or journey and requires a claim-matched fixture.
controls.repairCost: Correct the owning producer/schema and preserve prior evidence rather than rewriting it.
controls.carryingCost: Maintain twelve journeys, five packet templates, evidence roots, and lifecycle fixtures.
controls.reviewTrigger: Journey, producer, packet, evidence, authority, output, or external consequence change.
controls.retirement: Retire only when a smaller packet proves the same consequence and limitation without losing provenance.
controls.rollback: Restore the prior producer/schema and retain failed/superseded packets.

metricPlan.acceptedOutcome: Track accepted journey outcomes by candidate.
metricPlan.falsePositive: Track packets rejected despite matching observable evidence.
metricPlan.feedbackLatency: Measure run to actionable bounded receipt.

controls.nonClaim: Local proof contracts do not establish external actuality.

controls.failureClass: unused dependency, dead export, package boundary, or source-condition drift
controls.invariant: Knip, package exports, TypeScript projects, and build/tests keep live source and consumer boundaries coherent.
controls.admissionEvidence: Workspace typechecks/builds/tests plus Knip cover current package and export consumers.
controls.falsePositiveCost: A false positive requires tracing the real import/export consumer or generated entry.
controls.repairCost: Correct the package/export/config owner rather than add a blanket ignore.
controls.carryingCost: Maintain workspace projects, source conditions, manifests, and narrowly justified ignores.
controls.reviewTrigger: Dependency, package, export, generated entry, source condition, TypeScript, or build change.
controls.retirement: Retire an ignore when its consumer disappears; retire the control only if the package manager/compiler owns equal proof.
controls.rollback: Restore the prior manifest/export graph and consumer tests.

metricPlan.acceptedOutcome: Track accepted dependency/export removals and corrections.
metricPlan.falsePositive: Track justified live consumers initially reported dead.

controls.nonClaim: A clean dependency graph does not prove runtime or provider behavior.
